metric_value: Aberdeen structures coverage in matrix: by market initiative x supplier type (IPSP/ISV/OEM/VAR)
notes: Foundational framing document; January 1999

metric_name: Top IS professional services demand areas
metric_value: Windows 2000 infrastructure, ERP/Supply Chain, CIS/Data Knowledge, Education ranked as top four IS buyer demand areas per Aberdeen surveys
notes: Based on Aberdeen field surveys

metric_name: ERP market pace of technology introduction
metric_value: ERP solution technology introduction has accelerated over past several years; few corporations can stay current
notes: Reflects late-1990s ERP implementation boom

metric_value: EC services include Systems Design/Integration, Project Management, Lifecycle Management, Training
notes: Aberdeen definition of EC professional services scope

metric_value: ISVs (SAP/Baan/Oracle/PeopleSoft) increasingly building professional services forces to deploy their own software
notes: Trend toward ISV-led implementation services

metric_value: OEMs eroding reseller margins via internet-based direct sales; VARs/resellers turning to professional services as growth strategy
notes: Prescient: internet disintermediation of channel proven accurate

metric_name: CIS and Data Knowledge market growth
metric_value: CIS and Data Knowledge are among fastest-growing segments of Enterprise Business Applications (EBA) market
notes: Cited as comparable growth trajectory to ERP

metric_value: Research in 1999 focused on transition from classroom training to distance learning and technology-based training solutions
notes: Proved highly prescient; e-learning became mainstream within 5 years

metric_value: Distance learning provides greater flexibility at lower costs (financial and opportunity); internet/intranet delivery options
notes: Aberdeen assessment of distance learning value proposition

metric_name: IBM IT professional services strategic positioning
metric_value: IBM's E-Business initiative unifies all marketing, product, and services strategies under single theme
notes: Aberdeen notes IBM's unusually coherent message discipline

metric_value: IBM Global Services has more than 110000 skilled professionals deployed in 164 countries
notes: Cited as IBM competitive advantage over HP (~25000) and Compaq (~34000 post-Digital)

metric_value: $19 billion of $78 billion total IBM annual revenue
notes: Aberdeen notes OEM revenue inflation vs. IPSP revenue; $19B includes maintenance contracts

metric_value: Aberdeen estimates IBM derives ~$11.5 billion annually in product support revenues
notes: Aberdeen estimate based on large installed systems/software base

metric_value: $15.2 billion total revenue; approximately one-third from single client (GM)
notes: Aberdeen notes GM dependency as competitive vulnerability for EDS

metric_value: Hewlett-Packard has approximately 25000 professional services staff; number two OEM services provider
notes: Aberdeen comparative workforce analysis

metric_value: Compaq + Digital + Tandem workforce estimated at ~34000 professionals; strengthens enterprise-level and NT-based services
notes: Digital acquisition closed 1998; Tandem acquired 1997

metric_name: HP Unix vs NT services gap
metric_value: HP strong in Unix-oriented professional services but comparatively slow to build NT deployment services at scale
notes: Aberdeen assessment comparing HP vs IBM service portfolio

metric_value: ~500000 AS/400-class systems installed; ~100000 Unix systems; cited as IBM enterprise computing heritage
notes: Supports IBM outsourcing and integration positioning; AS/400 was mature, profitable installed base

metric_value: Cost per transaction minute dropped from ~$300 three years prior to <$50 on Unix/NT platforms as of 1999
notes: Cited as driving worldwide shortage of skilled IT professionals

metric_value: Commoditization created worldwide shortage of qualified IS professionals for deployment and lifecycle management
notes: Cited as major market driver for IBM Global Services growth

metric_value: IBM claims primary competitors are Andersen Consulting and EDS, not HP/Compaq
notes: Reflects IBM's self-positioning as IPSP/consulting competitor rather than hardware peer

metric_value: IBM likely world's largest provider of IT-related professional services and business strategic planning
notes: Aberdeen characterization; qualified due to revenue categorization differences

metric_value: IBM customers cite reliability as primary reason for purchasing IBM professional services; pricing secondary consideration
notes: Based on Aberdeen customer interviews

metric_name: IBM global services delivery consistency gap
metric_value: Some IBM customers cite inconsistent service delivery on global basis; IBM established centers-of-competency to address
notes: Aberdeen identifies execution gap despite IBM size advantage

metric_name: Aberdeen IT Professional Services practice coverage scope
metric_value: 4 practice areas covered in 1999: Windows 2000 Infrastructure, ERP/Supply Chain, CIS/Data Knowledge, Educational Services
notes: Structural overview document; defines Aberdeen's 1999 research agenda
